courses.subject: EPSY
courses.title: Applied Developmental Psychology

meetings.instructor: Professor WONG Pak Ho
Professor WONG Pak Ho
Professor WONG Pak Ho
Professor WONG Pak Ho
Professor WONG Pak Ho
Professor WONG Pak Ho
Professor WONG Pak Ho

courses.description: This course provides a framework for identifying learning and clinical issues which specifically occur during lifespan development from childhood through adolescence and young adulthood. It also allows EP trainees an opportunity to lead and participate in scholarly and didactic discussions of educational and clinical issues relevant to developmental psychology, and to the Hong Kong context.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MA Professional Educational Psychology
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of the Course, EP Trainees will be able to:
1. Demonstrate knowledge of specific issues in developmental psychology in the context of Hong Kong;
2. Integrate theories, research, and practice in developmental psychology; and
3. Identify and work with clients with the said issues in educational settings.

(Individual course teachers may update the learning outcome as and whenever necessary.)
courses.course_syllabus:
Self-Concept and Identity Development
Peer Relationships
Family Relationships
Love, Sex and Marriage
Challenges of the Cyberworld
Addictions
Motivation and Learning

(For reference only, please refer to the course outline of respective teacher for details.)
courses.feedback_evaluation:
Feedback from EP trainee will be collected via course-end evaluation. Continuous feedback, comments or questions are encouraged through communications and email exchanges.

(For reference only, please refer to the course outline of respective teacher for details.)

Durkin, K., & Schaffer, H. R. (2016). The Wiley handbook of developmental psychology in practice: Implementation and impact. UK: Wiley.

courses.subject: EPSY
courses.title: Cultural Diversity and Individual Differences: Implications for Learning and Practice

meetings.instructor: Professor WONG Pak Ho
Professor WONG Pak Ho
Professor WONG Pak Ho
Professor WONG Pak Ho
Professor WONG Pak Ho
Professor WONG Pak Ho
Professor WONG Pak Ho

courses.description: This course is designed to facilitate EP Trainees’ self-awareness of diversity issues in educational settings, which is of importance to the professional competency of educational psychologists. During the course, EP trainees are expected to actively engage in trainee-led discussion and experiential activities that increase their knowledge, sensitivity, critical thinking, and skills repertoire in handling diversity issues in their educational psychology practices.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MA Professional Educational Psychology
courses.course_syllabus:
Multicultural Competencies
Gender
Social-Economic Status
Ethnic Minorities
Sexual Minorities
Cultural-Sensitive Learning and Teaching
Cultural-Sensitive Interventions

(For reference only, please refer to the course outline of respective teacher for details.)

Feedback from EP trainee will be collected via course-end evaluation. Continuous feedback, comments or questions are encouraged through communications and email exchanges.

courses.subject: EPSY
courses.title: Consultation in Schools and Community: Theory and Practice

meetings.instructor: Professor WONG Pak Ho

courses.description: This course examines the role of educational psychologists as consultants and systems-change agents, whose charge is to strengthen the capacity of various interlocking systems (e.g., school and family) that have an impact on students’ well-being and development. Consultation theory, problem-solving models, and a variety of sequential consultation strategies to facilitate systems-change are explored, including data-based decision-making and target identification, solution generation, and solution implementation. The role of educational psychologists as external consultants in schools to facilitate crisis management and prevention is discussed.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MA Professional Educational Psychology
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of the Course, EP Trainees will be able to:
1. Display knowledge of school system and other settings (family and community), and use consultation theory and problem-solving models to create and promote a healthy and supportive learning environment for students.
2. Demonstrate abilities and skills to support and provide consultation to schools and communities based on the developmental needs of students, data-informed decision and the outcomes of evidence-based interventions.
3. Demonstrate abilities and skills in the transfer of knowledge and provide mentoring/ coaching to teachers regrading various prevention, intervention strategies and crisis management.
4. Demonstrate knowledge and skills to promote home-school-community collaboration and effectively engage in multi-discipline professional collaboration to promote the well-being of the students.

(Individual course teachers may update the learning outcome as and whenever necessary.)
courses.course_syllabus: (Please refer to the course outline of respective teacher for details.)
courses.feedback_evaluation:
Feedback from EP trainee will be collected via course-end evaluation. Continuous feedback, comments or questions are encouraged through communications and email exchanges.

(For reference only, please refer to the course outline of respective teacher for details.)

courses.description: This course is the third part for skills training. It serves as a platform for EP trainees to polish micro-skills that are required in their concurrent practicum. Specific focuses include case formulation, group facilitations, inter-disciplinary collaborations, consultations, and systems-intervention. This course also provides a forum in which experienced educational psychologists and allied professionals can share their specialities and expertise with EP trainees.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MA Professional Educational Psychology
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of the Course, EP Trainees will be able to:
1. Demonstrate skills in assessment, intervention, consultations and group facilitations proficiently;
2. Demonstrate abilities in reflecting on the experiences in the fieldwork; and
3. Provide services in accordance to professional ethics and legal standards.

(Individual course teachers may update the learning outcome as and whenever necessary.)
courses.course_syllabus:
Practicum orientation
Case formulation and feedback
Inter-disciplinary collaborations
Consultation skills
Group development and facilitation
Systems-intervention
Case presentation and discussion

(For reference only, please refer to the course outline of respective teacher for details.)

courses.description: This course is the last series of skills training. It serves as a platform for EP trainees to further polish micro-skills that are required in their concurrent practicum. It also provides them with opportunities to seek support and guidance for the challenges they come across in practicum. In addition, it is a forum in which educational psychologists and allied professionals from the field can share with the students their experience in difficult cases and latest development in intervention. EP trainees will also be guided to develop a personal career development plan for their long-term professional and personal growth.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MA Professional Educational Psychology
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of the Course, EP Trainees will be able to:
1. Demonstrate skills and knowledge in handling difficult situations at fieldwork;
2. Develop a career development plan for long-term professional and personal growth; and
3. Provide services in accordance to all professional ethics and legal standards.

(Individual course teachers may update the learning outcome as and whenever necessary.)
courses.course_syllabus:
Practicum orientation
Handling difficult clients
Communicating effectively in inter-disciplinary teams
Resolving ethical dilemmas in fieldwork
Career talk and sharing
Case presentation and discussion

courses.subject: EPSY
courses.title: Dissertation Research (I)

meetings.instructor:
Professor LAM Yu Hin, 
Professor LUK Sau Ha, 
Professor WONG Pak Ho

courses.description: This seminar is the first course in a two-course dissertation sequence. EP trainees participate in individual or small group tutorials that guide them in utilizing knowledge and skills in research methods and statistics to conduct a dissertation research project. Emphasis will also be on helping students appreciate the need for evidence-based practice, supported by program evaluation of efficacy and effectiveness as indicated by intervention research relevant to professional practice in educational psychology. This course will guide EP Trainees toward completion of a dissertation proposal.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MA Professional Educational Psychology
courses.learning_outcomes:
After taking this course, EP Trainees will be able to:
1. Demonstrate ability to critically evaluate research evidence in one area of Educational Psychology.
2. Identify research gaps and formulate research questions which can be addressed using appropriate research and analytical methods.
3. Write up a detailed research proposal for their final year dissertation.

(Individual course teachers may update the learning outcome as and whenever necessary.)
courses.course_syllabus: (Please refer to the course outline of respective teacher for details.)
courses.feedback_evaluation:
Feedback from EP trainee will be collected via course-end evaluation. Continuous feedback, comments or questions are encouraged through communications and email exchanges.

(For reference only, please refer to the course outline of respective teacher for details.)

courses.subject: EPSY
courses.title: Practicum (II)

meetings.instructor:
Dr. LAM Wing Chi, 
Dr. LUI Wan Hap, 
Dr. WONG Kathy, 
Mr. NGAN Wai Lam, 
Ms. LEE Man Wai

Dr. LAM Wing Chi, 
Dr. LUI Wan Hap, 
Dr. WONG Kathy, 
Mr. NGAN Wai Lam, 
Ms. LEE Man Wai

courses.description: Practicum provides opportunities for students to develop their abilities and competence in integrating theory and practice under professional supervision. Each EP Trainee is expected to have fieldwork training of a minimum of 1200 hours, with at least 600 hours in educational settings, for the whole practicum. The first practicum provides hands-on experience in relation to individual work and group work. It should include experiences in working with students, families and/ or teachers. The placement sites/ collaborating parties can include, but not limited to, the educational psychology service unit of the Faculty, and mentorship scheme under the MA(EP) program. In the second and third practicum, EP Trainees are supervised closely and regularly by qualified educational/ clinical psychologists in the field. The placements provide hands-on experience in relation to individual work, group work, and systems work, encompassing a wide range of student/ school needs, and they should include experiences in working with students, families, teachers, and improving the school system. The practicum settings include, but not limited to, preschools, primary schools, secondary schools, tertiary institutions and government departments.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MA Professional Educational Psychology
courses.learning_outcomes:
At the end of the course, EP Trainees will be able to:
1. Demonstrate adequate professional knowledge, abilities and skills in a broad array of assessment and intervention activities to facilitate individual or systems changes.
2. Provide educational psychological services consistent with legal and professional standards.
3. Effectively engage in home-school-community collaboration to promote students’ well-being.
4. Polish skills in professional report writing and record keeping for inform service and intervention decision.

(Individual course teachers may update the learning outcome as and whenever necessary.)
